## Description
A demonstration that good ingredients beat fancy technique. Forty minutes on the stove, virtually no work.

## Instructions
1. Empty the tomatoes (with their juice) into a medium saucepan. Crush them lightly with a wooden spoon.
2. Add the butter, both onion halves cut-side down, and the salt.
3. Simmer uncovered over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally and crushing the tomatoes further, for 40 minutes. The sauce will thicken and the butter will rise into a glossy slick.
4. Fish out the onion (or save it as a cook's snack). Taste and adjust salt.

## Notes
- Toss with hot pasta and a splash of pasta water; finish with grated Parmigiano.
- Don't substitute olive oil here — the butter is the point.

## Substitutions
- Cherry tomatoes in summer (use about 700 g; longer cook).

## Make-ahead
Refrigerate up to 5 days, freeze up to 3 months.
